Recovering Critical Silicon From the Ocean Floor.

We restore saltwater-corrupted drives and ROV telemetry logs retrieved from extreme bathypelagic pressures.

Abyssal Data Recovery operates at the extreme edge of digital forensics. Based in the maritime hub of Plymouth, we specialise exclusively in the extraction of data from solid-state drives, black boxes, and ROV sensor arrays that have been subjected to catastrophic deep-sea submersion. We do not handle spilled coffee on laptops; we rebuild silicon that has been crushed under six thousand metres of saltwater pressure and severe galvanic corrosion. When a tether snaps or a submersible breaches, we ensure the telemetry and visual logs survive.

Halting Galvanic Corrosion at the Source

At the core of our forensic laboratory is the Brine-Wash Desalination Protocol. When a solid-state drive is recovered from the ocean floor, the immediate threat is not just pressure, but the rapid crystallisation of marine salts which bridge electrical components and trigger runaway galvanic corrosion the moment the drive hits surface oxygen. We immediately submerge recovered silicon in a series of temperature-controlled, ultrasonic baths using laboratory-grade distilled water and specialised chemical displacement agents. This highly controlled cavitation process gently shakes microscopic salt deposits free from the printed circuit board without damaging the fragile surface-mount capacitors or the NAND flash memory chips. By halting the chemical degradation at a molecular level before attempting any electrical readouts, we boast an eighty-five percent success rate in stabilising drives that conventional data labs would classify as total, irreversible losses.

Bypassing Crushed Logic Controllers

The Hadal-Tier Drive Reconstruction service is our most extreme recovery protocol, engineered specifically for hardware subjected to the crushing hydrostatic pressures found below six thousand metres. At these depths, standard waterproof housings frequently fail, resulting in imploded drive casings and shattered internal logic controllers. When a standard SATA or NVMe controller chip is physically crushed, the data still resides within the microscopic floating gates of the NAND flash memory. Our engineers utilise precision micro-soldering under stereoscopic microscopes to bypass the destroyed controller entirely. We manually wire fine copper filament directly to the test points of the surviving NAND chips, extracting the raw hexadecimal data block by block. This raw dump is then computationally reassembled using custom algorithms to reconstruct the original file system, rescuing vital sonar topographies and structural integrity footage from the abyss.

Restoring Corrupted Benthic Visual Logs

Modern remotely operated vehicles produce terabytes of invaluable scientific and industrial data during a single dive. When a catastrophic umbilical tether failure occurs, the ROV is often lost to the seabed, alongside hours of un-transmitted 4K benthic video feeds and precise photogrammetry mapping. Our ROV Telemetry Rescue division specialises in decrypting and restoring these highly specific proprietary file formats once the vehicle is physically salvaged. Saltwater intrusion frequently corrupts the file allocation tables, turning high-definition video logs into unreadable digital static. We do not rely on commercial recovery software; instead, we manually rewrite the file headers and stitch the corrupted video frames back together using advanced hex editing techniques. Whether you are recovering a wrecked pipeline inspection drone or a lost marine biology array, we ensure your hard-won underwater intelligence is fully restored and immediately actionable.

The Wet-Transport Courier Protocol

When physical retrieval from the ocean floor is achieved, the hardware must remain submerged in the identical saline environment it was found in. Removing a deep-water solid-state drive into surface oxygen rapidly accelerates galvanic corrosion, effectively destroying delicate circuit trace wires within hours. We mandate the Wet-Transport Courier Protocol for all offshore engineers managing a recovery. Secure your damaged drive inside a sealed, rigid polymer container flooded completely with the ambient saltwater. Our logistics network maintains a dedicated courier pipeline running directly from major UK commercial ports—including Aberdeen, Southampton, and Falmouth—straight to our moored laboratory facility in Plymouth. Upon receipt, our engineering team immediately transfers the hardware into controlled liquid suspension, initiating a strict 24-hour triage diagnostic window. This timeline is critical; the initial assessment determines the structural integrity of the NAND gates before any desalination begins. Failure to adhere to wet-transport guidelines drastically reduces our ability to bypass shattered logic controllers.

Download Wet-Transport Instructions

Depth-Rated Recovery Packages

We enforce a strict, transparent pricing structure calibrated against the severity of hydrostatic pressure damage and saltwater intrusion. Every recovery operation requires an initial non-refundable laboratory diagnostic fee of £500, which is fully credited toward your selected extraction tier upon successful telemetry retrieval.

Continental Shelf Recovery

0 - 200 Metres
£3,500+

Standard saltwater cleaning, ultrasonic baths, and minor trace wire stabilisation for shallow-water submersions.

Bathypelagic Restoration

200 - 4,000 Metres
£8,200+

Advanced recovery requiring extensive board-level repairs, component replacement, and mitigation of micro-fractures.

Hadal-Tier Extraction

4,000+ Metres
£18,500+

Direct NAND reads, bespoke hexadecimal reassembly, and intensive micro-soldering to bypass completely imploded casings and shattered controllers.

Request a Lab Diagnostic

Certified for Classified Naval Recovery

Securing proprietary industrial mapping and sensitive military intelligence requires absolute physical and digital isolation. Abyssal Data Recovery is fully certified under ISO 27001 for Information Security Management, maintaining rigorous chain-of-custody documentation from the moment a wet drive enters our facility. We hold active Ministry of Defence (MoD) clearances, authorising our engineers to handle and reconstruct classified sonar logs, naval telemetry, and restricted maritime schematics. To guarantee total data sovereignty, our Plymouth laboratory operates exclusively as an offline, air-gapped environment. No recovered data ever touches a networked server or internet-connected workstation. All hex dumps, video restorations, and reconstructed file systems are processed on isolated cryptographic workstations. Upon successful extraction, your data is transferred to a secure, hardware-encrypted external drive and delivered via bonded courier. We do not retain redundant copies post-delivery, ensuring your intellectual property remains completely contained.

View Security Protocols

Verified Subsea Extractions

"The engineering team at Abyssal Data Recovery successfully retrieved three weeks of high-resolution photogrammetry data from our flooded drone housing. We assumed the telemetry was lost completely when the seals failed at 1,200 metres, but their micro-soldering bypass saved the entire pipeline mapping project."

Marcus Vance, Lead ROV Pilot, North Sea Pipelines

"When our autonomous research glider crashed into the seabed off the Hebrides, the internal solid-state drives sat submerged for nine days. Abyssal’s brine-wash desalination process stabilised the severely corroded memory chips, rescuing our entire season of benthic salinity recordings."

Dr. Sarah Hemlock, Director of Marine Archaeology, University of Southampton

"We experienced a catastrophic tether failure on a heavy industrial submersible. The logic controllers were crushed under 5,000 metres of pressure. Abyssal performed a direct NAND extraction, restoring the structural integrity video logs required for our insurance investigation."

David Aris, Chief Marine Surveyor, Deepwater Salvage Operations
Read Full Case Studies

Mission Control

Initiating a recovery requires precise operational details. Use our emergency lab dispatch form below to alert our engineering team before your hardware reaches the surface. You must specify the exact hardware type, the estimated depth of submersion in metres, and the total number of days the equipment has been exposed to saltwater. We require this data to prepare the correct chemical displacement agents and calibrate the ultrasonic baths prior to arrival.
